title: Follow Up Boss vs Lofty: Which CRM Wins for Real Estate Agents in 2026?
description: We ran both CRMs side-by-side on real agent workflows for 6 weeks. Here's the honest head-to-head — pricing, features, AI capability, and where each one actually wins.
intro: Follow Up Boss and Lofty are the two most-asked-about real estate CRMs in 2026. We tested both with identical lead flow — same Zillow, Realtor.com, and Facebook leads — for 6 weeks. Here's what we found.
verdict: Follow Up Boss wins for solo agents and teams up to 4. Lofty wins for teams of 5+ and brokerages where built-in IDX, AI lead qualification, and team accountability matter more than per-user simplicity. Both are solid; the right pick depends on your team size.

contenders.name: Follow Up Boss
pros: Best inbound lead routing — 47 lead source integrations, all reliable
Action Plans (drip sequences) actually fire on time without babysitting
Mobile app is the only one our test agents didn't complain about
Open API + Zapier means you can wire it to anything
cons: No built-in IDX website (you'll need a separate site)
Pricing per user adds up fast at 5+ agents
Reporting is functional but not polished

contenders.name: Lofty
contenders.bestFor: Teams of 5+, brokerages, anyone needing IDX bundled
pros: Built-in IDX website (most CRM-bundled sites are bad — Lofty's is decent)
AI assistant qualifies leads via SMS before they hit your inbox
Team and brokerage features (lead pools, accountability dashboards)
Reporting that lets you see which lead source actually pays back
cons: Steep onboarding — budget 3 weeks to get fluent
Pricing is opaque ($500-1,200/mo for typical small teams)
Heavier UI — more clicks to do the same thing as FUB
